name: claude-squared
description: Long-running, addressable Claude Code CLI sub-sessions as MCP tools — pairs that can recurse, persist context, and use native /compact and /skill commands via stream-json.
long_description:
Spawn named long-running Claude Code CLI sub-sessions ('pairs') that the calling Claude session can address by name. Each pair runs `claude --print --resume <uuid>` under the hood, giving it true recursion (it can spawn its own Agent sub-tasks), persistent context across turns, per-pair specialization (system prompt, allowed tools, MCP scope pinned at create), native /compact and /context via stream-json, and FIFO-queued sends.

**Why use a pair vs the built-in Agent tool?**
- Pairs survive across many of your turns; sub-agents are one-shot.
- A pair can recurse — it has the full Agent tool by default, so it can spawn its own scouts.
- Specialized config persists: `system_prompt_append`, `allowed_tools`, MCP scope are pinned at create.
- Multiple pairs in parallel give you addressable fan-out; the sync `pair_send` is FIFO-queued per pair.

**Key tools** (17 total, all prefixed `pair_`):
- Lifecycle: `pair_create`, `pair_adopt`, `pair_forget`
- Communication: `pair_send` (sync), `pair_send_async` + `pair_poll`
- Inspection: `pair_list`, `pair_info`, `pair_transcript`, `pair_actions`, `pair_context`
- Mutation: `pair_update`, `pair_clear`, `pair_compact`
- Skills: `pair_invoke`
- Custom agents: `pair_agent_define`, `pair_agent_list`

**State on disk** lives in `~/.claude/pairs/` (registry + profiles + archive + async tasks) and `~/.claude/agents/` (custom agent definitions, visible globally).

tools.name: pair_create
tools.description: Spawn a new long-running named pair (Claude Code CLI sub-session) with optional pinned config (system prompt, allowed tools, MCP scope, cwd).

tools.name: pair_adopt
tools.description: Register an existing claude session UUID as a pair so it becomes addressable through this MCP.

tools.name: pair_forget
tools.description: Remove a pair from the registry; archives transcript by default.

tools.name: pair_send
tools.description: Send a message to a pair and block for the response. Concurrent sends to the same pair queue FIFO. Response includes context-usage status with warnings ≥60%.

tools.name: pair_send_async
tools.description: Send a message asynchronously; returns task_id immediately.

tools.name: pair_poll
tools.description: Check status of an async task: running | done | failed.

tools.name: pair_list
tools.description: List all registered pairs (short form).

tools.name: pair_info
tools.description: Get full configuration + stats for one pair, including transcript path.

tools.name: pair_transcript
tools.description: Tail the last N user/assistant turns from a pair's session JSONL.

tools.name: pair_actions
tools.description: Discoverability: list curated actions, plus (if name given) the slash commands and agent types available in that specific pair.

tools.name: pair_update
tools.description: Update mutable settings (model, effort, permission_mode, allowed_tools, purpose).

tools.name: pair_clear
tools.description: Reset a pair's conversation history by rotating to a fresh session_id; pinned config preserved, old transcript archived.

tools.name: pair_compact
tools.description: Native /compact via stream-json. Optional steering prompt focused on conversation arc + binding rules + in-flight state (defer technical detail to .md docs).

tools.name: pair_context
tools.description: Invoke /context in the pair to get a rich token-usage breakdown (model, totals, categorized usage).

tools.name: pair_invoke
tools.description: Invoke a skill in the pair by name (translates to /<skill> via stream-json).

tools.name: pair_agent_define
tools.description: Define a custom claude-code agent in ~/.claude/agents/ — visible to all Claude sessions globally.

tools.name: pair_agent_list
tools.description: List all custom agents under ~/.claude/agents/.

tools.name: pair_settings_get
tools.description: Show current pair MCP settings: writable defaults (model/effort/permission_mode/persistent/extra_dirs), file paths, and read-only env knobs.

tools.name: pair_settings_set
tools.description: Set per-user defaults for new pairs. bypassPermissions refused as default (foot-gun); pass per-call. Supports model='match-parent' for JSONL-based parent detection.

tools.name: pair_settings_reset
tools.description: Remove ~/.claude/pairs/defaults.json; subsequent pair_create uses only hardcoded fallbacks (Opus/xhigh/auto).
